pairs.question: What is the Tirerack.com Battle on the Bricks and which racing series does it belong to?
pairs.answer: The Tirerack.com Battle on the Bricks is an IMSA WeatherTech SportsCar Championship event held at the Indianapolis Motor Speedway. It is a stand-alone race now, but was originally a support race for the Brickyard 400 as part of the 'Kroger Super Weekend at the Brickyard' from 2012 to 2014.

pairs.question: How did the format of the Tirerack.com Battle on the Bricks evolve over time?
pairs.answer: Originally a support race for the Brickyard 400 from 2012 to 2014, the event was part of the Grand Am Rolex Sports Car Series in its first two years. It moved to the United SportsCar Championship in 2014 before going on hiatus. The race was revived in 2023 as a stand-alone event in the WeatherTech SportsCar Championship, running for two hours and forty minutes. In 2024, it expanded to a six-hour endurance race, becoming one of the major endurance races on the IMSA schedule. The race will revert to a two-hour forty-minute format in 2026 and be replaced as a six-hour race by the IMSA SportsCar Weekend at Road America.

pairs.question: What notable changes occurred in the Tirerack.com Battle on the Bricks from 2023 to 2026?
pairs.answer: In 2023, the race was revived as a stand-alone event with a two-hour forty-minute duration. In 2024, it became a six-hour endurance race, joining other major endurance races like Daytona and Sebring. The race is scheduled to return to a two-hour forty-minute format in 2026 and will be replaced as a six-hour Endurance Cup race by the IMSA SportsCar Weekend at Road America.

pairs.question: Which racing series did the Tirerack.com Battle on the Bricks belong to during its original run (2012–2014)?
pairs.answer: The Tirerack.com Battle on the Bricks was part of the Grand Am Rolex Sports Car Series in 2012 and 2013, and then moved to the United SportsCar Championship in 2014 before going on hiatus.

pairs.question: Why was the Tirerack.com Battle on the Bricks revived in 2023?
pairs.answer: The race was revived in 2023 as part of the WeatherTech SportsCar Championship to bring back a stand-alone event at the Indianapolis Motor Speedway, following its hiatus after 2014.

pairs.question: What is the significance of the Tirerack.com Battle on the Bricks in the IMSA schedule?
pairs.answer: The Tirerack.com Battle on the Bricks is one of the major endurance races on the IMSA schedule, joining events like the 24 Hours of Daytona, 12 Hours of Sebring, and Petit Le Mans. Its inclusion emphasizes its importance in the endurance racing calendar.

pairs.question: How long is the Tirerack.com Battle on the Bricks scheduled to be in 2026?
pairs.answer: In 2026, the race is scheduled to revert to a two-hour forty-minute format, after being a six-hour endurance race in 2024 and 2025.
